intro: Enckea tenuis, also known as the Enckea vine, is a tropical plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, providing relief from various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Enckea tenuis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Enckea tenuis is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, as well as in modern herbal medicine, where it is often combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
